items.q: An object moves in a circle at constant speed. Is it accelerating?
items.correct: Yes — its direction is constantly changing, so its velocity changes; centripetal acceleration points toward the centre of the circle.
items.misconception: No — constant speed means no acceleration.
items.random_wrong: Only if it speeds up or slows down on the circle.
items.why: Students equate acceleration with speeding up, missing that any change in velocity (including direction) constitutes acceleration.

items.q: Why does Faraday's law include a minus sign (Lenz's law)?
items.correct: The induced current flows in a direction that opposes the change in flux that produced it — a consequence of energy conservation.
items.misconception: The minus sign is a mathematical convention with no physical meaning.
items.random_wrong: The minus sign means the current flows backwards through the wire.
items.why: Students treat the minus sign as a notational quirk rather than encoding Lenz's law, the deep statement that induced effects oppose their cause.

items.q: If one EM wave has a higher frequency than another, what is true about its wavelength?
items.correct: It is shorter — wavelength and frequency are inversely related (c = f x lambda).
items.misconception: It is also longer, because both increase together.
items.random_wrong: It is unchanged — only the speed differs.
items.why: Students treat all wave properties as moving together (more of one = more of the other), missing the fixed-speed constraint that ties them inversely.

items.q: A book on a high shelf has lots of energy in its:
items.why: It's gravitational store. “kinetic store” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Petrol stores energy in which form?
items.why: Correct answer: chemical. “thermal” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many joules of work are done when a 10 N force moves an object 3 m?
items.why: The real figure is 30 J. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
